title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator designed to run Gemma models for on-device AI inference. The board ships as a kit with I/O peripherals including a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, with open-source demos covering live translation, natural language control, and multimodal on-device applications.
writeup: This video covers Google's Coral Board, a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator built to run Gemma models for on-device inference. It matters because it pushes neural network workloads onto edge hardware rather than the cloud. The board ships as a kit with I/O peripherals: a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs. Open-source demos cover live translation, natural language control, and multimodal on-device applications.
